'''Norval''' was a dark-haired [[Human/Legends|Human]] youth who studied [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] lore on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] circa [[39 BBY/Legends|39 BBY]]. When his professor, [[Murk Lundi]], took a sabbatical to scour [[Kodai]] for a long-lost [[Sith holocron/Legends|Sith holocron]], Norval was disheartened to not accompany his mentor.

Devising his own plan to obtain the artifact, Norval trailed Lundi to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]], followed by another pupil, [[Omal]]. It was only Omal's bravery that prevented Norval from succeeding in his quest: once Lundi found the [[Holocron/Legends|holocron]], Omal's actions lost it in a deep geyser.

Lundi was imprisoned for ten [[Standard year/Legends|years]], eventually returning to Kodai in [[29 BBY/Legends|29 BBY]] under the supervision of [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Master]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and his [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]], [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]]. However, Norval acquired the holocron and fled before being found by the Jedi. He made his way to Lundi's native [[Ploo system/Legends|Ploo system]], with the pair of Jedi on his heels. There, he would rendezvous with an [[Carnor Jax's father|agent]] of [[Order of the Sith Lords|the Sith]] to deliver the holocron. Obi-Wan Kenobi boarded Norval's ship and confronted him. Norval briefly dueled Kenobi using a rudimentary [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] he had constructed, but was no match for the Jedi. Kenobi took the holocron and returned to his Padawan.

The Sith ship turned on Norval, with the intent to prevent the Jedi from recovering the holocron. Norval, once a bright, promising youth at the center of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], perished in a a hail of [[Laser/Legends|laser]] fire that obliterated his vessel.
